user_interaction_guidelines.overview: Guidelines for interacting with users when creating JDBC connectors. Focus on gathering information efficiently and providing clear guidance.

initial_assessment.title: Determine What User Knows
initial_assessment.description: Assess how much information the user has provided about their database

what_to_ask.question: Confirm database details and gather any specific requirements
what_to_ask.use_database_examples: Reference the MySQL example from database_examples.xml

scenario.user_input: Create a connector for PostgreSQL with SSL support

what_to_ask.question: Confirm if standard configuration is acceptable or if other customizations needed

scenario.user_input: Create jdbc_oracle connector with driver oracle.jdbc.OracleDriver
scenario.what_you_know: Database type, connector name, and driver class

scenario.user_input: Create a connector for Vertica database
scenario.what_you_know: Database type not in common examples

question_strategy.principle: Ask only what you need to know
Provide intelligent defaults based on database type
Offer 2-4 specific suggestions for each question

ask_followup_question.question: I'll create a MySQL connector. Should I use the standard configuration or do you need any customizations?

follow_up.suggest: Use standard MySQL configuration (driver: com.mysql.cj.jdbc.Driver, port: 3306)
Standard configuration with SSL support enabled
Custom configuration - I'll specify the details

example_bad_question.bad_approach: What is the driver class? What is the port? What is the URL template? Do you need SSL? What about schemas?

using_database_examples.when_to_use: User mentions a common database (MySQL, PostgreSQL, Oracle, SQL Server, DB2, MariaDB)

how_to_use.step: Reference the appropriate example from database_examples.xml
Use the example values as defaults
Only ask about customizations if user indicates special needs

using_database_examples.example:
User says: "Create a PostgreSQL connector" Your response: "I'll create a PostgreSQL connector using the standard configuration: - Driver: org.postgresql.Driver - Default port: 5432 - With SSL support option
Proceeding with connector creation..."

progress_communication.principle: Keep user informed at each major step
Show clear progress through the workflow

phase.name: dsl_creation
phase.announcement: Creating DSL configuration file for {database_name} connector...

phase.name: code_generation
phase.announcement: Generating connector skeleton code... Running: ./gradlew generateJavaJdbc

phase.name: customization
phase.announcement: Customizing connector with DSL configuration... Running: ./gradlew customizeJdbcConnector

phase.name: test_generation
phase.announcement: Generating comprehensive test suite... Running: ./gradlew generateJdbcTests

error_communication.principle: Offer to retry or adjust approach

example_error_handling.error: Gradle task fails with "DSL file not found"
example_error_handling.good_response:
The customization task couldn't find the DSL file. This is likely because: 1. The path needs ../ prefix since DSL is in parent directory 2. The file name doesn't match exactly
I'll retry with the correct path: -PdslFile=../DSL-jdbc-{name}.json

final_summary.title: Provide Clear Summary

what_to_include.item: What was created (connector name, database type)
Current status (built successfully, ready for testing, etc.)

final_summary.example_summary:
✅ MySQL JDBC connector created successfully!
Created files: - DSL-jdbc-mysql.json - Configuration file - sdk-gen/subprojects/jdbc_mysql/ - Connector source code - TestPropertyValidation.java - Comprehensive test suite - CUSTOMIZATION_SUMMARY.md - Documentation
Build status: ✅ Compiled successfully
Next steps: 1. Add JDBC driver dependency to build.gradle: implementation 'mysql:mysql-connector-java:8.0.33'
2. Update test credentials in TestPropertyValidation.java with real database details
3. Run tests: cd sdk-gen && ./gradlew :jdbc_mysql:test
4. Build Flight server image when ready: ./gradlew dockerBuild

next_steps_clarity.principle: Explain why each step is needed

step_template.why: The connector needs the database driver to connect
step_template.how: Edit sdk-gen/subprojects/{connector_name}/build.gradle Add to dependencies section: implementation '{driver_artifact}'

case.name: unknown_database
case.description: User wants connector for database not in examples

approach.step: Offer to research if user is unsure

ask_followup_question.question: I'll create a connector for {database_name}. I need some information about the JDBC driver. What is the fully qualified driver class name?

follow_up.suggest: I'll look it up in the database documentation
I'm not sure - can you help me find it?

case.name: user_has_dsl_file
case.description: User already has a DSL configuration file

approach.step: Ask for the DSL file path
Read and validate the DSL file

case.name: modifying_existing_connector
case.description: User wants to modify an existing connector

approach.step: Identify what needs to be changed
Update the DSL file if it exists

tone_and_style.guideline: Be direct and technical, not conversational

good_examples.example: Creating MySQL connector with standard configuration...
Running generateJavaJdbc task to create connector skeleton
Connector built successfully. JAR created in build/libs/

bad_examples.example: Great! I'll help you create a MySQL connector!
Sure thing! Let me get started on that for you!
Awesome! The connector is ready to go!

principle.name: Minimize Questions
principle.description: Use database examples and intelligent defaults to avoid unnecessary questions

principle.name: Batch Related Operations
principle.description: Complete related steps together rather than asking for confirmation at each step

principle.name: Provide Context
principle.description: Explain what you're doing and why, so user understands the process

principle.name: Anticipate Needs
principle.description: Proactively address common next steps and potential issues
